title: ammi majus subsp. intermedium
intro: Ammi majus subsp. intermedium is a subspecies of the large sweet cicely plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, known for its fern-like foliage and delicate white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative effects, which make it useful for treating digestive issues and relieving pain. The health benefits of Ammi majus subsp. intermedium include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. The herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
